global_gate.reason: Implementation and deployment changes stay blocked until every spine surface is ready in order: auth_core, then bears_gateway, then cd_deploy_stage.

promotion_rules.rule: bears_gateway cannot open for auth-dependent implementation before auth_core implementation_gate is open; cd_deploy_stage cannot open for CI behavior changes before auth_core and bears_gateway are open.
Every surface must route through the plugin-owned specialist role before product, platform, or deployment edits. Missing coverage is ROLE_COVERAGE_BLOCKER.
Secret values, production data, CI variables with values, tokens, keys, and raw private configs must never be read, printed, committed, or documented. Stable secret reference names are allowed.

blocking_evidence.detail: Repo-local AGENTS requires docs MCP startup, readiness, and repo brief; current verified result is 401 Unauthorized, so auth_core repo work stays fail-closed until ready evidence or explicit scoped operator override exists.
Endpoint boundary matrix is not documented for public-auth, authenticated-user, internal-service, and admin classes.

required_evidence_before_open: KB startup contract and readiness status are accessible and ready, or an explicit scoped operator override is recorded.
Repo-root AGENTS.md, SPEC.md, and requirements.md stay present at /srv/bears/dev/platform while canonical_path remains the neutral auth package subtree.
Endpoint boundary matrix covers public-auth, authenticated-user, internal-service, and admin route classes.
Automated auth tests cover success, unauthorized, forbidden, and replay/invalid bootstrap paths without secret values.

legacy_source_refs.allowed_use: tenant legacy source, migration source, compatibility evidence, or test fixture only; never universal core default, namespace owner, required input, or required dependency
legacy_source_refs.deprecated_projects_refs: Legacy compatibility input only; not canonical source authority.

blocking_evidence.detail: Auth-dependent gateway implementation is blocked until auth_core baseline evidence opens first.
Route-by-route class inventory is not yet proven against implementation.
WebSocket handshake auth expectation and rejection behavior are not yet proven by tests.

required_evidence_before_open: auth_core implementation_gate is open or the operator records a scoped auth override.
Route-by-route inventory maps every exposed route to public, public-with-fake-unauth, bearer-auth, wb-token-derived, or websocket.
Authorization, X-Request-ID, X-Organization-Id, and X-Organization-Inn propagation rules are proven by tests.
Stable 401, 403, 504, and WebSocket auth rejection behavior is covered by automated tests.

required_shape: gateway config or code rollback reference
smoke evidence for auth and tenant propagation

legacy_source_refs.allowed_use: tenant legacy source, migration source, compatibility evidence, or test fixture only; never universal core default, namespace owner, required input, or required dependency
legacy_source_refs.deprecated_projects_refs: Legacy compatibility input only; not canonical source authority.

blocking_evidence.detail: Shared CI behavior changes require explicit consumer graph, rollback execution path, and post-deploy evidence before opening.
Rollback shape is documented, but no validated rollback job or execution path is implemented.
Current post-deploy evidence shape is documented but not proven as broad runtime readiness evidence.

required_evidence_before_open: auth_core and bears_gateway implementation gates are open, or an explicit scoped operator override narrows the deploy-only task.
Every shared template has a current consumer graph or an explicit exclusion rationale.
Rollback execution path is validated with non-secret previous artifact or config reference.
Post-deploy evidence proves the promoted service state without exposing secrets or production data.

required_shape: previous image tag or config commit

legacy_source_refs.allowed_use: tenant legacy source, migration source, compatibility evidence, or test fixture only; never universal core default, namespace owner, required input, or required dependency
legacy_source_refs.deprecated_projects_refs: Legacy compatibility input only; not canonical source authority.
